passageParts.content: In recent years, many universities have tried to help students develop stronger learning habits. Some programs begin by asking students to reflect on how their attention is
during a normal study day. The purpose is not to blame them, but to make invisible habits
. Once students see when and why they lose focus, they can make small changes that are easier to

than ambitious plans.

One common problem is that students often confuse being busy with being productive. They may spend hours in the library, yet much of that time is
by switching between messages, videos, and unfinished notes. Research suggests that frequent switching can
mental energy and make it harder to remember what has just been read. For this reason, some teachers encourage students to study in short blocks and to set a clear

before each block begins.

Another useful habit is reviewing mistakes soon after practice. A wrong answer is not merely a sign of failure; it is a
to the part of the process that needs attention. If a student chose an answer because it looked familiar, the real problem may be weak
of the passage rather than poor vocabulary. If the student ignored a word such as however or therefore, the mistake may come from missing the author's

.

Good learning plans also leave room for rest. Without enough sleep, students may be able to sit at a desk, but their ability to
information is reduced. This does not mean that effort is unimportant. Rather, effort works best when it is
with recovery. In this sense, a realistic schedule is more valuable than one that looks impressive but cannot be

.

Technology can either support or weaken these habits. A phone can provide dictionaries, timers, and practice materials, but it can also
attention away from the task. The difference often lies in whether the student has made a
decision about how the tool will be used. For example, placing the phone across the room during a reading session may seem simple, yet it can

the number of interruptions.

Ultimately, better study is less about dramatic change than about repeated choices. Students who improve usually do not become different people overnight. They learn to notice patterns, test adjustments, and
what works. Progress is therefore built through a series of modest actions that
over time. When students understand this, they are less likely to wait for perfect motivation and more likely to
with the next manageable step. That step may be reviewing one error, reading one paragraph carefully, or planning one quiet hour. Small as it is, such action can
confidence and turn study from a vague hope into a practical routine that can be

blanks.clue: attention is affected by habits，表示注意力受到影响。
blanks.explanation: 空格与 attention 构成被动关系，affected 最自然。

optionAnalysis.A: affected 表示受到影响，符合语境。
optionAnalysis.B: achieved 通常表示达成目标，不能搭配 attention is achieved。
optionAnalysis.C: extracted 表示提取，语义不符。
optionAnalysis.D: restored 表示恢复，后文没有恢复语境。

blanks.commonMistake: 只看单词正负色彩，没看主语 attention 和谓语搭配。
blanks.strategy: 先判断空格与前后名词的搭配，再比较选项词义。

blanks.clue: 前文 invisible habits，后文 see when and why，对应 make ... visible。
blanks.explanation: 目标是让看不见的习惯变得可见。

optionAnalysis.A: visible 与 invisible 构成反义复现。
optionAnalysis.B: private 表示私人的，不符合 see 的线索。

blanks.commonMistake: 忽略反义复现 invisible/visible。
blanks.strategy: 看到前后出现反义词或同义词，要优先考虑复现关系。

blanks.clue: small changes 更容易坚持，而 ambitious plans 难以持续。
blanks.explanation: sustain 表示维持、坚持，符合改变习惯的语境。

optionAnalysis.A: sustain 表示持续维持。

blanks.commonMistake: 没有结合 easier than ambitious plans 的对比。
blanks.strategy: 比较级句子要看比较对象，判断动作方向。

blanks.clue: switching between messages, videos, and unfinished notes 表示学习被打断。
blanks.explanation: 时间被不同任务打断，所以 interrupted 合适。

optionAnalysis.A: interrupted 表示被打断。

blanks.commonMistake: 只看到 spend hours，忽略 yet 后的转折。
blanks.strategy: yet 后面通常给出真实问题，是解题重点。

blanks.clue: frequent switching 会消耗 mental energy。
blanks.explanation: drain mental energy 表示消耗精力。

optionAnalysis.A: save 与后文 harder to remember 矛盾。

blanks.commonMistake: 没有根据 harder to remember 判断负向语义。
blanks.strategy: 看后果句判断选项正负方向。

blanks.clue: set a clear ... before each block begins。
blanks.explanation: set a goal 是固定搭配，表示设定目标。

optionAnalysis.A: set a reward 可说但不如 goal 符合学习块。
optionAnalysis.B: set a clear goal 搭配自然。

blanks.commonMistake: 没有识别 set a clear goal 的固定搭配。
blanks.strategy: 看到 set a clear，要优先考虑 goal/task/standard 等搭配。

blanks.clue: wrong answer 指向需要注意的过程环节。
blanks.explanation: 错题是一个信号，提示哪里需要复盘。

optionAnalysis.A: threat 太消极。
optionAnalysis.B: signal 表示信号，符合后文 to the part。

blanks.commonMistake: 把 wrong answer 只理解成 failure，没有看 not merely 后的转折。
blanks.strategy: not merely A; it is B 中 B 是作者强调点。

blanks.clue: looked familiar 与 passage rather than vocabulary 对比，说明文章理解弱。
blanks.explanation: 选择熟悉选项可能说明没有真正理解文章。

optionAnalysis.A: memory 过泛。
optionAnalysis.B: location 不搭配 weak of the passage。
optionAnalysis.C: understanding of the passage 自然。
optionAnalysis.D: confidence 不符合 rather than poor vocabulary。

blanks.commonMistake: 没看 rather than 后的排除关系。
blanks.strategy: rather than 前后通常构成正确归因和错误归因的对比。

blanks.clue: however/therefore 是作者论证逻辑信号。
blanks.explanation: 忽略转折和因果词，会错过作者论证思路。

optionAnalysis.A: argument 表示论证。
optionAnalysis.C: schedule 与本句逻辑词无关。

blanks.commonMistake: 忽略逻辑词导致作者思路断裂。
blanks.strategy: 完形中逻辑词常提示空格前后的语义关系。

blanks.clue: sleep 不足会降低保留信息的能力。
blanks.explanation: retain information 表示保留、记住信息。

optionAnalysis.A: retain information 是自然搭配。

blanks.commonMistake: 没有识别 retain information 的常见搭配。
blanks.strategy: 先看宾语 information，再选能自然搭配的动词。

blanks.clue: effort works best when it is ... with recovery。
blanks.explanation: 努力和恢复需要平衡。

optionAnalysis.A: balanced with recovery 表示与恢复平衡。
optionAnalysis.B: confused with 表示混淆，不合语境。

blanks.commonMistake: 只看到 effort，忽略 recovery 的配合关系。
blanks.strategy: with 后面的名词常决定动词或形容词搭配。

blanks.clue: realistic schedule 比 impressive but cannot be ... 更有价值。
blanks.explanation: 计划需要能维持，maintained 最符合。

optionAnalysis.A: maintained 表示维持执行。

blanks.commonMistake: 没有看 realistic 与 cannot be maintained 的对比。
blanks.strategy: but 后常出现否定评价，与前文形成对比。

blanks.clue: phone can also ... attention away from the task。
blanks.explanation: pull attention away from 表示把注意力拉走。

optionAnalysis.A: pull attention away 搭配自然。

blanks.commonMistake: 没有识别 away from 的搭配提示。
blanks.strategy: 介词短语 away from 常提示转移、拉开类动词。

blanks.clue: whether the student has made a ... decision about how the tool will be used。
blanks.explanation: 是否有意识地决定如何使用工具决定其利弊。

optionAnalysis.A: conscious decision 表示有意识的决定。

blanks.commonMistake: 只看 decision，不看 about how the tool will be used。
blanks.strategy: 抽象名词 decision 前常考形容词搭配。

blanks.clue: 把手机放远会减少打扰次数。
blanks.explanation: placing the phone across the room 的结果是减少 interruptions。

optionAnalysis.A: reduce the number of interruptions 符合语境。

blanks.commonMistake: 没有从例子推导目的。
blanks.strategy: 例子后的 can 常引出该行为的效果。

blanks.clue: notice patterns, test adjustments, and ... what works。
blanks.explanation: 发现有效方法后要保留、坚持。

optionAnalysis.A: keep what works 表示保留有效做法。

blanks.commonMistake: 没有看并列动词 notice/test/keep 的递进。
blanks.strategy: 并列结构中空格要和前后动词方向一致。

blanks.clue: series of modest actions ... over time。
blanks.explanation: 小行动随时间累积形成进步。

optionAnalysis.A: accumulate over time 表示逐渐累积。

blanks.commonMistake: 忽略 over time 的时间积累线索。
blanks.strategy: over time 常提示 gradually/accumulate/develop 一类词。

blanks.clue: not wait for perfect motivation，而是从下一步开始。
blanks.explanation: more likely to begin with the next manageable step。

optionAnalysis.A: begin with 搭配自然。
optionAnalysis.D: compare with 不符合后文 step。

blanks.commonMistake: 没看 less likely/more likely 的对比。
blanks.strategy: A rather than B 或 less/more likely 常形成反向选择。

blanks.clue: small action can ... confidence。
blanks.explanation: build confidence 表示建立信心。

optionAnalysis.A: build confidence 是固定搭配。
optionAnalysis.B: damage confidence 与文章积极方向相反。

blanks.commonMistake: 没有看 confidence 的固定搭配。
blanks.strategy: 抽象名词 confidence 常与 build/increase/boost 搭配。

blanks.clue: practical routine that can be ...，呼应 repeated choices。
blanks.explanation: 学习从希望变成可重复的实际习惯。

optionAnalysis.A: repeated 与 routine 和 repeated choices 呼应。

blanks.commonMistake: 没有抓住结尾对 repeated choices 的主旨复现。
blanks.strategy: 结尾空常回扣全文主旨词。

discourseAnalysis.mainIdea: 文章说明高效学习依赖可观察、可维持、可重复的小习惯，而不是夸张的计划。

structure.summary: 通过反思注意力状态，让学习习惯可见。
区分忙碌和高效，指出频繁切换会消耗精力。
及时复盘错题，把错误看作学习过程的信号。
学习计划需要休息和恢复配合。
技术工具既能支持学习，也可能分散注意力。
进步来自可重复的小行动。

contextClues: 固定搭配 set a goal

coreWords.sentence: Students often confuse being busy with being productive.
Their ability to retain information is reduced.
Small changes are easier to sustain.

familiarWordsWithNewMeanings.sentence: Frequent switching can drain mental energy.
